description_long: This object is a round coconut with a rough, brown exterior forming the lower half and a smooth, glossy white layer covering the upper half, resembling a coconut with its top cut off and filled with a white substance. The brown husk has a natural, uneven texture, while the white upper portion appears thick and slightly domed, with a shallow depression at the center. The transition between the brown and white layers is irregular, mimicking the way coconut flesh meets the shell. There are no visible openings, stems, or attachments on any side. The object is compact and squat, with a nearly symmetrical, cylindrical profile. Its overall appearance is clean and stylized, with no visible markings or damage.
description: A round coconut with a rough brown husk and a glossy white top, featuring a shallow central depression and an irregular transition between the two layers.
description_view_0: The coconut shows a prominent white glossy top with a central depression and a rough brown base, with the white layer dipping lower on the left side.
description_view_1: The white top appears more evenly distributed, and the brown husk is visible around the lower half, with a smooth transition between layers.
description_view_2: The white layer dips slightly lower on the right, and the brown husk is more exposed on the left, with the central depression still visible.
description_view_3: The white top covers more of the coconut, with the brown husk forming a narrower band at the base and the transition line appearing higher on the left.
attribute_husk_pattern: The brown husk displays a rough, matte texture with subtle, irregular mottling and no distinct fiber directionality or dense fiber clusters. The surface appears slightly uneven, with natural variations in color and texture typical of a coconut shell.
